experiments.purpose: Heap drain(while:_:) exclusivity conflict: 6 variants tested, ~Escapable does not help, manual peek/take loop remains best safe pattern
do throws(ComplexGenericType) compiler crash: REFUTED — crash was stale incremental build artifact, no typealias workaround needed
experiments.statusDetail: API/package drift; experiment premise no longer expressible against current toolchain or peer-package API

experiments.purpose: Sync/async overload resolution: Swift unconditionally picks async in async context. Correct behavior — sync overload is for non-async callers. No API change needed.
Async.Channel as IO arm replacement: Unbounded viable (1.14x vs current), Bounded rejected (4.96x). Three general Bounded improvements identified.
Stored @Sendable closures with consuming ~Copyable params: all 5 variants pass. @Witness pattern can use consuming Owned in _register. Closure literals need explicit throws(E) annotation.

~Sendable (SE-0518) for thread-confined classes: 8 variants tested. ~Sendable compiles, region analysis allows safe transfers (V3/V4), correctly rejects Sendable containers (V7). Replaces @unchecked Sendable.
experiments.statusDetail: API/package drift; experiment premise no longer expressible against current toolchain or peer-package API

experiments.purpose: Reproduce alleged actor-state visibility bug via custom serial executor + inline-fallback runSynchronously. 5 variants, 1003 attempts in -c release with -sil-disable-pass=CopyPropagation. Bug NOT reproduced — minimal pattern is insufficient. swift-io's release-mode failure must depend on a more specific code shape.
experiments.statusRaw: NEGATIVE — bug needs swift-io's exact context
experiments.result: NEGATIVE — bug needs swift-io's exact context

experiments.purpose: Validate io_uring → eventfd → epoll integration path for single-thread architecture. Submit NOPs, block on epoll_wait, drain CQ. 1K/10K/100K ops all confirmed. 100K NOPs in 13ms (~0.13µs/op).

mapError returning sending IO<NewError>: 5 variants. V1 (consume self), V2 (@Sendable wrappers), V4 (sending helper), V5 ([self] literals) all FAIL — region is intrinsic to closure captures. Only V3 succeeds, but it requires LeafError: Sendable + @Sendable stored closures (i.e., a witness shape change, not a mapError technique).

@Witness(.mock) with borrowing/consuming ~Copyable parameters: REFUTED — stale disabled comment at IO.swift:92–98; Swift 6.3 compiler infers ownership through macro-generated (_, _) placeholders. IO.fake() could now be macro-generated.

Witness. composition operators (Recording, Scope, Values, Sequence, Cycle) with non-underscored @Witness storage: all five operators compose transparently, V4 "property wins" collision class doesn't appear.

Theoretically-perfect IO algebra as ~Copyable linear computation type IO<Environment, LeafError, Value>. pure/map/flatMap/mapError/catchAll/orElse/recover/provide/local/andThen. No @Sendable anywhere. Three load-bearing value-type Sendable constraints (pure, fail, ask) documented as Swift 6.3 region-checker requirements.
